alerts.title: US and Israel reportedly strike Iranian vessels at Larak Island in Strait of Hormuz; 3 killed
alerts.summary: The Straits Times and Crypto Briefing report a joint US-Israeli strike on Iranian vessels near Larak Island in the Strait of Hormuz, with three reported killed. Larak sits astride the world's most critical oil chokepoint and hosts IRGC Navy fast-attack craft. The strike occurred hours after Iranian officials arrived in Qatar to resume talks, marking a sharp escalation despite Trump's claim a deal is 'largely negotiated.'

alerts.title: US CENTCOM confirms 'self-defense' strikes on targets in southern Iran
alerts.summary: NBC News, Jerusalem Post and CENTCOM confirm renewed American strikes in southern Iran, framed as troop-protection actions. Strikes are geolocated around 31.7-32.1°N / 52.7-53.2°E and came just hours after senior Iranian officials landed in Qatar for talks. Threat level on the dashboard is now 9/10 ('CRITICAL — IMMINENT ESCALATION'); Brent has firmed to $94.40.

alerts.title: Iran says deal 'not imminent'; Khamenei demands enriched uranium stay in country
alerts.summary: BBC reports Tehran publicly contradicting Trump's claim that an agreement — including reopening the Strait of Hormuz — is 'largely negotiated.' A Reuters exclusive cites Iranian sources saying Supreme Leader Khamenei insists the 60% enriched uranium stockpile must remain inside Iran, while Russia's IAEA envoy calls US demands an NPT violation. Breakout estimate holds at ~1-2 weeks.

alerts.title: US sentiment turns sharply negative as 'deal,' 'war,' 'oil prices' dominate news cycle
alerts.summary: VADER sentiment on US news flips to 55% negative / 36% positive, with 'trump' (65), 'iran' (48), 'deal' (38), 'war' (11), 'oil/prices' (12 combined) leading the word cloud. The shift coincides with CENTCOM strikes, the Larak Island incident, and Brent crude at $94.40 — indicating mainstream coverage has pivoted from negotiation optimism to active-conflict framing.

alerts.title: Republican hawks publicly revolt against Trump's emerging Iran framework
alerts.summary: Middle East Eye reports Republican hawks calling Trump's draft Iran deal a 'nightmare for Israel,' while Israeli opposition leader Yair Lapid (via PBS) labels it 'bad for the region.' The New Yorker frames any plausible deal as 'a humiliation' for Trump. Combined with Iran rebuilding IRGC missile cities faster than expected, the domestic political backlash narrows the White House's room to de-escalate.

analysis_note: Despite ongoing Qatar talks, the theater has crossed into active kinetic exchange — confirmed US CENTCOM strikes in southern Iran and a reported US-Israeli naval strike at Larak Island (3 killed) coincide with Iran publicly rejecting Trump's deal framing and Khamenei refusing to surrender enriched uranium, pushing the dashboard threat level to 9/10.
